channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Black Hat USA
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Hims & Hers warns of data breach after Zendesk support ticket breach
item.description: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers Health is warning that it suffered a data breach after support tickets were stolen from a third-party customer service platform. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
item.description: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025, following a two-year period of minimal targeting in the region. The campaign has bee...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple Breaks Precedent, Patches DarkSword for iOS 18
item.description: Even organizations with users unwilling or unable to adopt iOS 26 can now protect themselves from a severe mobile OS-cracking tool....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Die Linke German political party confirms data stolen by Qilin ransomware
item.description: The Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for an attack against Die Linke ('The Left'), forcing an IT systems outage at the political party, and threatening sensitive data leak. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Details Cookie-Controlled PHP Web Shells Persisting via Cron on Linux Servers
item.description: Threat actors are increasingly using HTTP cookies as a control channel for PHP-based web shells on Linux servers and to achieve remote code execution, according to findings from the Microsoft Defender...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
item.description: As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Evolution of Ransomware: Multi-Extortion Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Multi-extortion ransomware relies on stolen data to pressure victims with public leaks. Penta Security explains how its D.AMO platform keeps exfiltrated files encrypted and useless to attackers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Picking Up 'Skull Vibrations'? Could Be XR Headset Authentication
item.description: "Skull vibration harmonics generated by vital signs" can be used to sign in to VR, AR, and MR headsets, according to emerging research....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
item.description: Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Chainguard Unveils Factory 2.0 to Automate Hardening the Software Supply Chain
item.description: The rebuilt Chainguard platform adds deeper security designed to continuously reconcile open-source artifacts across containers, libraries, Actions and skills....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM Can Now Ingest Microsoft Defender Telemetry
item.description: Once CrowdStrike's nemesis, Microsoft is now a collaborator. A shared interest in Formula 1 helped thaw the years-long fierce rivalry....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft still working to fix Exchange Online mailbox access issues
item.description: Microsoft is investigating and working to resolve Exchange Online mailbox access issues that have intermittently affected Outlook mobile and macOS users for weeks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UNC1069 Social Engineering of Axios Maintainer Led to npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The maintainer of the Axios npm package has confirmed that the supply chain compromise was the result of a highly-targeted social engineering campaign orchestrated by North Korean threat actors tracke...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture
item.description: The next major breach hitting your clients probably won't come from inside their walls. It'll come through a vendor they trust, a SaaS tool their finance team signed up for, or a subcontractor nobody ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New SparkCat Variant in iOS, Android Apps Steals Crypto Wallet Recovery Phrase Images
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new version of the SparkCat malware on the Apple App Store and Google Play Store, more than a year after the trojan was discovered targeting both the mobile...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Man admits to locking thousands of Windows devices in extortion plot
item.description: A former core infrastructure engineer has pleaded guilty to locking Windows admins out of 254 servers as part of a failed extortion plot targeting his employer, an industrial company headquartered in ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Drift Loses $285 Million in Durable Nonce Social Engineering Attack Linked to DPRK
item.description: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1, 2026. "Earlier today, a malic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft now force upgrades unmanaged Windows 11 24H2 PCs
item.description: Starting this week, Microsoft has begun force-upgrading unmanaged devices running Windows 11 24H2 Home and Pro editions to Windows 11 25H2. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CERT-EU: European Commission hack exposes data of 30 EU entities
item.description: The European Union's Cybersecurity Service (CERT-EU) has attributed the European Commission cloud hack to the TeamPCP threat group, saying the resulting breach exposed the data of at least 29 other Un...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
item.description: AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Claude Code leak used to push infostealer malware on GitHub
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting the recent Claude Code source code leak by using fake GitHub repositories to deliver Vidar information-stealing malware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Not Toying Around: Hasbro Attack May Take 'Weeks' to Remediate
item.description: The company's 8-K filing notes "unauthorized access" and that it has activated business continuity plans and taken some systems offline....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A large-scale credential harvesting operation has been observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Serv...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Security Bosses Are All-In on AI. Here's Why
item.description: CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its ...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Drift loses $280 million North Korean hackers seize Security Council powers
item.description: The Drift Protocol lost at least $280 million after a threat actor took control of its Security Council administrative powers in a planned, sophisticated operation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: RSAC 2026: AI Dominates, But Community Remains Key to Security
item.description: As AI took center stage at this year's conference, experts debated automation, oversight and the evolving role of human intelligence in cybersecurity — despite the US government's notable absence....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Residential proxies evaded IP reputation checks in 78% of 4B sessions
item.description: Researchers warn that residential proxies used to route malicious traffic are a big problem for IP reputation systems, as there is no clear distinction between attackers and legitimate users. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco Patches 9.8 CVSS IMC and SSM Flaws Allowing Remote System Compromise
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Adversaries Exploit Vacant Homes to Intercept Mail in Hybrid Cybercrime
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting vacant homes as "drop addresses" to intercept mail and enable fraud. Flare shows how postal services and fake identities are abused to turn mail into a fraud vector. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
